note: NORM node VERIFIED SOUND and -- contrary to the suspicion that it might illegitimately treat B_sa as an honest JB algebra -- it is correctly grounded on the ORDER structure, NOT the o product. The order-unit norm ||X||_ou=inf{t:-t1<=X<=t1} depends ONLY on the inherited cone (B_sa)_+=B_sa cap B(H)_+ and unit 1, NOT on o. The JB-algebra facts (GT-bhsa-jc :2300 byte-matched, GT-jc-is-jb :2320 byte-matched, GT-jb-orderunit-3310 :2552 byte-matched) are applied to the AMBIENT V=B(H)_sa (which IS an honest unital JB algebra) solely to obtain 'on V, order norm = operator norm'. B_sa then inherits the ORDER (not the product); since t1+-X in B_sa for X in B_sa, the inf over B_sa = inf over V, so ||X||_ou=||X||_{B(H)}. (i) order-unit, (ii) Archimedean, (iii) proper cone all correctly inherited from V. This matches def-eps-jb-algebra's design point (order/unit/norm exact, only product carries defect). The fact that B_sa's o is only approx-Jordan is IRRELEVANT to the norm -- correctly so. Sound.
